features.summary: Broadcom reported consolidated net revenue of $12 billion for Q1 FY2024, a 34% increase year-on-year, driven by a 153% growth in infrastructure software revenue, largely due to the acquisition of VMware. Semiconductor solutions revenue grew 4% year-on-year to $7.4 billion, with AI revenue quadrupling to $2.3 billion, offsetting declines in broadband and server storage segments. The company reiterated its fiscal 2024 guidance for consolidated revenue of $50 billion, representing 40% year-on-year growth.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued strong bookings at VMware, projecting software revenue to grow to $20 billion for FY2024. Semiconductor revenue is anticipated to grow mid- to high single digits year-on-year, with AI revenue now expected to represent 35% of total semiconductor revenue, exceeding previous estimates. The company remains committed to maintaining a disciplined approach to capital allocation and debt repayment throughout the fiscal year.
features.risks: Broadcom highlighted potential risks related to cyclical downturns in broadband and server storage markets, with revised expectations for revenue declines in these segments. Additionally, the company noted that actual results could differ materially from forward-looking statements due to various risk factors outlined in their SEC filings. The ongoing integration of VMware and the performance of its software offerings also present execution risks.
features.key_drivers: Key drivers of growth include the strong demand for AI-related products, particularly custom AI accelerators and networking solutions, which are expected to continue fueling revenue increases. The successful integration and upselling of VMware's products, particularly the VMware Cloud Foundation, are also critical to achieving the projected revenue targets. Management emphasized the importance of maintaining strong relationships with hyperscale customers and leveraging their existing technology to drive future growth.
